acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: docs/WRITEUP.md section 1 reproduces the Client/UI -> API Gateway -> gRPC -> Go Storage Engine / Python Agent layer diagram, module tables for engine/ and agents/, and the bidirectional call-graph note verbatim-consistent with docs/HLD.md section 3 (diagram, module tables, and bidirectionality note match line-for-line in substance).

acceptance_criteria_check.criterion: Novelty vs. GraphRAG/RAPTOR/MemGPT per HLD.md section 6
acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: docs/WRITEUP.md section 2 bullets for GraphRAG, RAPTOR, MemGPT/Letta, and HiveMind's contribution are near-identical restatements of docs/HLD.md section 6 with no added or exaggerated claims; write-up explicitly states 'no additional or stronger claims are made'. Verified by diffing both sections directly.

All 9 rows (3 checkpoints x 3 arms) of recall/precision/judge-score/cost-per-1k-queries in docs/WRITEUP.md section 3 were cross-checked against results/run-001/live_benchmark_results.json field-by-field; every value matches after correct 3-decimal (or 2-decimal for the precision-growth aside) rounding. actual_spend_usd ($0.1677), cost_cap_usd ($2.00), wall_clock_minutes_approx (35), llm/judge model (openai/gpt-4o-mini via openrouter), corpus size (32 docs), and queries-per-checkpoint (32) all match results/run-001/run-metadata.json exactly. No fabricated or misleadingly-rounded numbers found.

All 4 bullets independently verified against source: (1) fsync-bound WAL scaling claim (54ms/append, 40->200 workers 5x, 2400->4800 appends 2x) matches engine/loadtest/split_race_scale_test.go's package doc comment verbatim; (2) OCI 'unverified against a live OCI account' / bash -n only claim matches deploy/oci/README.md lines 7-9 verbatim; (3) free-list ceiling of 32,704 pages / (4096-8)*8 formula matches docs/LLD/catalog.md lines 191-204 verbatim; (4) prefix-only candidate search gap (all-stopword query -> empty pool, requires non-prefix primitive to close) matches docs/LLD/query-agent.md lines 95-145 verbatim.

findings.finding: All 4 required sections present and each independently verified against primary sources (HLD.md, benchmark JSON, LLD docs, loadtest source, deploy README). No section is missing or thin.

Document does not spin the benchmark results as a HiveMind win. Section 3 explicitly states plain vector RAG leads on raw recall and judge score at every checkpoint, and frames HiveMind's faster precision growth and flat per-query cost as the only positives, immediately caveated as 'a single run on a small corpus, not a claim of overall superiority.' This is the correct, non-exaggerated reading of the underlying data.

Architecture section matches docs/HLD.md section 3 structurally and in module responsibilities (catalog/btree/mvcc/split/graph/wal/rpc for engine/, ingestion/query/llm/eval for agents/); gRPC-not-REST rationale and bidirectional call graph (engine split -> agent ProposeSplit) both correctly reproduced.
N/A. Change is a single new markdown file (docs/WRITEUP.md, 157 lines); no code, config, contract, or dependency changes. No regression surface.
N/A. Pure documentation change, no secrets or credentials referenced or introduced.

Document embeds a data table copied from results/run-001/live_benchmark_results.json rather than generating it programmatically; if a future benchmark run overwrites run-001's results without updating this doc, the write-up could silently drift out of sync. Non-blocking since the write-up itself flags this ('Results will vary run to run; rerun with python -m eval.run_live_benchmark for a fresh sample') and results/run-001/ is presented as an immutable historical run, not a live-generated artifact.
Test spec was manual review; this is inherently satisfied by the acceptance-criteria checks above. No automated test applicable to a markdown-only change.
